title: Brain Protocol v2 forget receipt
description: The user-visible receipt produced after every forget-on-disconnect purge (BP-02 §5.4). The purge runs in dependency order (BP-02 §5.2); the audit then scans every table that can carry provenance — derived-memory stores included — and the result MUST be zero (BP-02 §5.3). The receipt is shown to the user in plain language, retained in the journal, and the derived_memories line is always explicit — derived memory is where forgetting fails silently. These are the minimum fields; systems MAY add more.

trigger.description: A 401/403 on an outbound call is the other side revoking you: treat it as a disconnect and trigger the same purge, audit and receipt (BP-02 §5.5).

erased.description: Per-type counts erased. derived_memories is always an explicit line.

keys_destroyed.description: BP-07's cryptographic-forgetting line: grant keys destroyed and tokens revoked, with timestamp. A receipt without it is incomplete (BP-07 §3.6, TCK T-SEC-09).

audit.description: The post-purge scan of every provenance-bearing table under the BP-02 §5.1 predicate (source, origin_chain, provenance — applied transitively).

rows_remaining.description: The result MUST be zero. A non-zero result is not a receipt; it is an incident.

backup_window_days.description: The published backup window (BP-02 §5.6). Any restore from backup replays the forget log and re-passes the audit before serving traffic.
